title: Party
description: Parties, addresses, and contact information. A Party is any legal entity or individual that participates in the private markets ecosystem — GPs, LPs, fund administrators, borrowers, lenders, brokers, and service providers.

Address.description: Physical or mailing address of a party. Used for legal correspondence, tax jurisdiction determination, and regulatory reporting. Example: {"line1": "345 Park Avenue", "city": "New York", "state_province": "NY", "postal_code": "10154", "country": "US"}

line1.description: Primary street address including building number. Example: '345 Park Avenue'

line2.description: Secondary address line for suite, floor, unit, or c/o information. Omit if not applicable. Example: 'Suite 3200'

city.description: City or municipality name. Example: 'New York'

state_province.description: State, province, or administrative region. Use standard abbreviations where available (e.g., 'NY' for New York, 'ON' for Ontario). Omit for countries without sub-national divisions. Example: 'NY'

postal_code.description: Postal or ZIP code in the format used by the country. Examples: '10154' (US), 'EC2N 1HQ' (UK), '100-0005' (Japan)

country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code. Determines tax jurisdiction and regulatory regime. Example: 'US'

ContactInfo.description: Contact details for an individual associated with a party. Used for investor relations, deal correspondence, capital call notifications, and operational communications. Example: {"name": "Jane Smith", "email": "jane.smith@apollo.com", "phone": "+1-212-555-0100", "title": "Managing Director, Investor Relations"}

name.description: Full name of the contact person. Use the name they go by professionally. Example: 'Jane Smith'

email.description: Business email address. Must be a valid email format (RFC 5322). This is the primary channel for official communications including capital call notices and reporting. Example: 'jane.smith@apollo.com'

phone.description: Phone number in E.164 international format preferred ('+' followed by country code and number, no spaces). Domestic formats are accepted but discouraged for cross-border operations. Example: '+12125550100'

title.description: Professional job title or role at the organization. Helps identify the appropriate contact for different types of communication (e.g., IR vs. legal vs. operations). Example: 'Managing Director, Investor Relations'

PartyType.description: Classification of a party's role in the private markets ecosystem. GP: General Partner — manages the fund and makes investment decisions. LP: Limited Partner — passive investor who commits capital. FUND_ADMIN: Fund Administrator — handles NAV calculations, investor reporting, capital call processing. CUSTODIAN: Holds and safeguards assets on behalf of investors. PLACEMENT_AGENT: Intermediary that helps raise capital from LPs. LEGAL_COUNSEL: Law firm providing legal advisory services. AUDITOR: Firm performing financial audits. BORROWER: Entity that borrows under a credit facility. LENDER: Entity that provides credit. BROKER: Intermediary in secondary market transactions. OTHER: Any role not covered above. Example: 'GP'

name.description: Full legal name of the entity as registered. This should match the name on legal documents (LPAs, credit agreements, etc.). Example: 'Apollo Global Management, Inc.'

short_name.description: Abbreviated or commonly known name used in day-to-day communication and UI display. Example: 'Apollo'

party_type.description: The primary role this party plays. A single legal entity may appear as multiple Party records if it operates in different capacities (e.g., a firm that is both a GP and an LP in different funds). Example: 'GP'

external_ids.description: References to this party in external systems. Use for cross-referencing with LEI registries, CRM systems, compliance databases, or portfolio management platforms. Example: [{"system": "LEI", "value": "5493001KJTIIGC8Y1R12"}]

address.description: Primary business or registered address. Used for legal notices, tax jurisdiction, and regulatory filings.

contacts.description: Key contact persons at this organization. Include at minimum the primary relationship contact. For LPs, include the person who handles capital call funding and the one who receives reporting.
